mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE crosses 4.5%, or signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through earnings revisions.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day returns, or interaction term IC < 0.01 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the industrial production index reflect rising factory utilization and capacity expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, or signal direction reverses when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4% monthly are followed by positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or Rank IC remains negative.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms labor market tightening, boosting corporate revenue expectations and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 60-day returns, or interaction effect vanishes in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts indicate rising consumer confidence and construction sector activity, transmitting positive momentum to S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases show no statistically significant positive correlation with 5-day forward returns over a 2-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Three consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal supply-side contraction and weakening economic momentum, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Consecutive INDPRO declines are followed by positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances, or signal loses predictive accuracy during contraction phases.
